fault_hypothesis: The non-overlap check on line 69 uses inclusive comparisons (`<=`, `>=`), which incorrectly classifies adjacent intervals that share an endpoint as disjoint. The conditions should use strict inequalities (`<`, `>`) to correctly flag these boundary cases as overlaps.

spec.expr: (c_end <= start or c_start >= end) == (c_end < start or c_start > end)

constraints.intent: The implemented non-overlap logic must be equivalent to the correct logic that uses strict inequalities for endpoints.
If a loop iteration completes without returning, the intervals must have been strictly disjoint.
The candidate interval should not merely touch the current reservation, as this constitutes an overlap that the buggy logic misses.
